title: Methods should avoid having identical implementations
description: Methods should not have identical implementationsAnalyze your codeadaptability-distinctmaintainabilityCode SmellconfusingduplicatesuspiciousWhy is this an issue?Two methods having the same implementation are suspicious. It might be that something else was intended. Or the duplication is intentional, which becomes a maintenance burden.private const string CODE = "secret"; private int callCount = 0; public string GetCode() { callCount++; return CODE; } public string GetName() // Noncompliant: duplicates GetCode { callCount++; return CODE; }If the identical logic is intentional, the code should be refactored to avoid duplication.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

never_trigger_on: Override methods (required to implement same pattern)
logic.reasoning: This rule detects methods with identical implementations that indicate code duplication. It compares normalized method bodies to find exact duplicates that should be refactored into common methods or helper functions. The rule excludes legitimate cases like interface implementations, override methods, and simple accessors where identical implementations are expected or required.

should_trigger: Methods with same logic but different parameter names
should_not_trigger: Methods delegating to common helper functions
